pattern: Stripping the separators reduces the task to greedily peeling three digits at a time off a plain digit string, with a special two-block split reserved for whatever is left over.
hints: First discard every space and dash so you are working with a plain run of digits, since the original grouping carries no meaning.
Repeatedly take a block of 3 digits off the front while more than 4 digits remain, so you never end up needing a block of length 1.
Once 4 or fewer digits remain, handle the leftover directly: 2 digits become one block, 3 digits become one block, and exactly 4 digits split into two blocks of 2, then join every block with dashes.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, ends with a block of 3
statement example, ends splitting 4 leftover digits
statement example, ends with a leftover block of 2
minimum length, exactly two digits and no separators
four digits must split into two blocks of two, never one block of four
digits only, long enough for several full blocks of three plus a two-digit tail

stress.description: random digits interleaved with random separators, near the length bound
